steps.title: Identify the relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1 gives a general definition: languages no longer spoken are extinct. Sentence 2 names Umbrian, a specific case fitting that definition.

steps.title: Pick the transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is example. The blank needs a transition signaling that Umbrian is a specific instance of the general claim about language extinction.
  • Choice A — In conclusion,: signals a final summary wrapping up an argument; a single definition sentence isn't an argument requiring a conclusion, so fails. ✗
  • Choice B — Therefore,: signals a logical inference drawn from evidence, but Umbrian's extinction isn't inferred from the definition—it's just an example of it, so fails. ✗
  • Choice C — Admittedly,: signals conceding a point that works against the writer's position; nothing is being conceded here, so fails. ✗
  • Choice D — For example,: signals a specific instance of a general claim, fitting the move from the definition to Umbrian as a case, so fits. ✓
D is correct.
